zh.Jülich, Rheinland, Prussia, Germany: 海因里希_海普洛文次爾利
zh.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern於1346年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇庫爾斯騰圖姆 普法爾茲。她和Wilhelm II. von Berg至少有9個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1415年11月30日在她的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪瑟爾多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern于1346年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇庫爾斯騰圖姆 普法爾茲。她和Wilhelm II. von Berg至少有9个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1415年11月30日在她的家乡享年69岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪瑟爾多福。

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a pet form of   Bayer  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

en.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern was born in 1346, in Palatinate, Holy Roman Empire. She had at least 9 sons and 3 daughters with Wilhelm II. von Berg. She died on 30 November 1415, in her hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Düsseldorf, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern est née en 1346, à Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Saint-Empire romain. Elle a eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles avec Wilhelm II. von Berg. Elle est décédée le 30 novembre 1415, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.Ravensberg, Bad Doberan, Mecklenburg-Vor: Ravensberg, Kreis Bad Doberan, Rostock, Deutsche Demokratische Republik
de.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern wurde 1346 in Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren. Sie hatte mit Wilhelm II. von Berg mindestens 9 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. November 1415 im Alter von 69 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayernは1346年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチクルフuルステントゥム プファルズで生まれた。彼女はWilhelm II. von Bergとの間に少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1415年11月30日に故郷で69歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンドuセルドルフにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern(은)는 1346년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 커프u르스투 p팔즈에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Wilhelm II. von Berg(와)과 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1415년 11월 30일 그녀의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 드u셀도르프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern nasceu em 1346, em Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Ela teve pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas com Wilhelm II. von Berg. Ela faleceu em 30 de novembro de 1415, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.Ravensberg, Bad Doberan, Mecklenburg-Vor: равенсбирг, крис бад добиран, ростокк, Восточная Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Anna Von Bayern родилась в 1346 г. в местности курфuрстентум пфалз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич. У неё было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери от Wilhelm II. von Berg. Она умерла 30 ноября 1415 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности дuсселдорф, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.
